docs.title: Projet Pokemon
docs.text:
Projet Pokemon Binome Hajdarevic Hugo et Tarlier Romain C++

Interactables Pokemons

docs.title: Types
docs.text: Name Description EntrainersFactory @brief Factory class for loading different types of trainers from CSV files. PokemonFactory @brief Pokemon Factory is used as a class that is designed to avoid mistakes made by users UI @brief Controls global UI I/O actions.

docs.text: Name Description output @brief Reads all players from a CSV file and constructs Joueur instances. output @brief Reads all Gym Leaders from a CSV file and constructs LeaderDeGym instances. output @brief Reads all Master Trainers from a CSV file and constructs MaitrePokemon instances. instance @brief Constructs the singleton Pokémon factory. m @brief Retrieves a loaded Pokémon by its name. output @brief Returns all Pokémon currently loaded in the factory.

docs.text: Name Description file @brief Loads Pokémon definitions from a CSV file into the factory.

docs.title: instance
docs.text:
static PokemonFactory instance

@brief Constructs the singleton Pokémon factory. Initializes the internal storage for loaded Pokémon. @brief Retrieves the global instance of the Pokémon factory.

Return Reference to the singleton PokemonFactory.

docs.title: m
docs.text:
auto& m

@brief Retrieves a loaded Pokémon by its name.

name The name key used when loading from CSV. Return A copy of the corresponding Interactables::Pokemon.

Note

If the name is not found, prints an error and exits(1).

docs.title: output
docs.text:
std::vector<Interactables::Joueur> output

@brief Reads all players from a CSV file and constructs Joueur instances.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing player data. The file is expected to have a header line followed by lines of the form: name,pokemon1,pokemon2,...,pokemon6. Return A std::vector of Interactables::Joueur objects populated from the CSV. Exits the program with code 1 if the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: output
docs.text:
std::vector<Interactables::LeaderDeGym> output

@brief Reads all Gym Leaders from a CSV file and constructs LeaderDeGym instances.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing gym leader data. The file is expected to have a header line followed by lines of the form: name,gymName,badge,pokemon1,...,pokemon6. Return A std::vector of Interactables::LeaderDeGym objects populated from the CSV. Exits the program with code 1 if the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: output
docs.text:
std::vector<Interactables::MaitrePokemon> output

@brief Reads all Master Trainers from a CSV file and constructs MaitrePokemon instances.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing master trainer data. The file is expected to have a header line followed by lines of the form: name,pokemon1,pokemon2,...,pokemon6. Return A std::vector of Interactables::MaitrePokemon objects populated from the CSV. Exits the program with code 1 if the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: output
docs.text:
std::vector<Interactables::Pokemon> output

@brief Returns all Pokémon currently loaded in the factory.

Return A std::vector containing copies of every loaded Pokémon.

docs.title: file
docs.text:
std::ifstream file(path)

@brief Loads Pokémon definitions from a CSV file into the factory.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing Pokémon data. The first line is treated as a header and skipped. Each subsequent line must have the form: name,type1,type2,hp,attack,attackDamage.

Note

On I/O failure or malformed file, prints an error and exits(1).

docs.title: EntrainersFactory
docs.text:
class EntrainersFactory

@brief Factory class for loading different types of trainers from CSV files.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Name Description getJoueursFromCSV @brief Loads player (Joueur) data from a CSV file. getLeadersFromCSV @brief Loads gym leader (LeaderDeGym) data from a CSV file. getMaitresFromCSV @brief Loads master trainer (MaitrePokemon) data from a CSV file.

docs.title: getJoueursFromCSV
docs.text:
static std::vector<Interactables::Joueur> getJoueursFromCSV(const std::string& path)

@brief Loads player (Joueur) data from a CSV file.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing player data. The first line is treated as a header and skipped. Each subsequent line must have the form: name,pokemon1,...,pokemon6. Return A vector of Interactables::Joueur instances constructed from the file. @throws std::exit(1) if the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: getLeadersFromCSV
docs.text:
static std::vector<Interactables::LeaderDeGym> getLeadersFromCSV(const std::string& path)

@brief Loads gym leader (LeaderDeGym) data from a CSV file.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing gym leader data. The first line is treated as a header and skipped. Each subsequent line must have the form: name,gymName,badge,pokemon1,...,pokemon6. Return A vector of Interactables::LeaderDeGym instances constructed from the file. @throws std::exit(1) if the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: getMaitresFromCSV
docs.text:
static std::vector<Interactables::MaitrePokemon> getMaitresFromCSV(const std::string& path)

@brief Loads master trainer (MaitrePokemon) data from a CSV file.

path Filesystem path to the CSV file containing master trainer data. The first line is treated as a header and skipped. Each subsequent line must have the form: name,pokemon1,...,pokemon6. Return A vector of Interactables::MaitrePokemon instances constructed from the file. @throws std::exit(1) if the file cannot be opened.

class UI

docs.title: boxed
docs.text:
static std::string boxed(const std::string& text, const std::string& align = "left", // "left", "center", "right" const std::string& upper_left = "╭", const std::string& upper_right = "╮", const std::string& lower_left = "╰", const std::string& lower_right = "╯", const std::string& vertical_border = "│", const std::string& horisontal_border = "─" )

Note

Expect 1len strings for {upper,lower}_{left,right} and {vertical,horisontal}_border

docs.title: Types
docs.text: Name Description AEntrainer Maximum Team Size Current number of Pokemon in team @brief Adds a Pokemon to the trainer's team pokemon : The pokemon to add Return : True if the Pokemon was successfully added (team not full) false otherwise @brief Removes a Pokemon from the trainer's team by it's index index : Zero-based position of the Pokemon to remove Return : True if removal succeeded, false otherwise @brief Retrieves a Pokemon from the team by its index index : Zero-based position of the Pokemon to return Return : A copy of the Pokemon at the given index @brief Gets the attack damage of a team mem `` : Return : @brief Abstract base trainer providing common implementation of IEntrainer. Manages a team of up to six Pokémon, offers default add/remove, battle logic, and stream output. Concrete trainers should override interaction behavior and may specialize attack damage. AInteragir @brief Performs the interaction behavior. IEntrainer @brief Interface for a Pokemon Trainer capable of managing a team of Pokemon Defines the basic operations for naming the trainer and adding, removing, retrieving POkemon and querying their attack damage Interagir @brief Interface for any interactable entity. Joueur @brief Represents the player, tracking badges and battle record. LeaderDeGym @brief Represents a Gym Leader who awards a badge upon defeat. MaitrePokemon @brief Represents a Master Trainer with boosted attack damage. Pokemon @brief Represents a Pokémon with attributes such as name, types, HP, and attack.

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Name Description cities @brief Custom interaction monologue for the Master Trainer upon battle. fightOver @brief Adds a Pokémon to this trainer’s team if there is available space. isLeader @brief Constructs a Gym Leader with a name, gym name, badge, and six Pokémon. nickname @brief Gets the Pokémon’s name. pokemon @brief Gets the attack damage of the Pokémon at the given index, or 0 if the Pokémon is fainted. pokemon @brief Records a defeat against another trainer. typeAttcker1 @brief Default constructs a Pokémon with placeholder values.

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Name Description operator<< @brief Restores the Pokémon’s HP to its maximum value. operator<< @brief Swaps two Pokémon in the team by their indices.

docs.title: cities
docs.text:
std::vector<std::string> cities

@brief Custom interaction monologue for the Master Trainer upon battle. Chooses a random city and displays a dramatic ASCII-art defeat sequence.

docs.title: fightOver
docs.text:
bool fightOver

@brief Adds a Pokémon to this trainer’s team if there is available space.

pokemon The Pokémon instance to add to the team. Return True if the Pokémon was successfully added; false if the team is already at maximum capacity. @brief Removes a Pokémon from this trainer’s team by its index. index Zero-based position of the Pokémon to remove. Return True if the Pokémon was successfully removed; false if the index is invalid or out of range. @brief Retrieves a reference to the Pokémon at the specified index in the trainer’s team. index Zero-based position of the Pokémon to retrieve. Return Reference to the Pokémon at the given index. Behavior is undefined if the index is out of range. @brief Returns all Pokémon currently on this trainer’s team. Return Const reference to the vector containing all team Pokémon. @brief Initiates and runs a turn-based battle against another trainer, displaying announcements, alternating attacks, and removing KIA Pokémon. other The opposing trainer to fight against. Return True if this trainer wins the battle (has more surviving Pokémon at the end), false if the opponent wins.

docs.title: isLeader
docs.text:
const LeaderDeGym* isLeader

@brief Constructs a Gym Leader with a name, gym name, badge, and six Pokémon.

name The leader’s name. gymnase The name of the gym they oversee. badge The badge awarded when defeated. p1..p6 Six Pokémon to form the leader’s team. @brief Gets the badge name awarded by this Gym Leader. Return Const reference to the badge string. @brief Gets the name of the gym this leader oversees. Return Const reference to the gym name string. @brief Checks whether this Gym Leader has been beaten. Return True if the leader has been marked as beaten. @brief Marks this Gym Leader as beaten (badge can be claimed). @brief Custom interaction when the player defeats the Gym Leader. Displays a defeat monologue and awards the badge. @brief Constructs a Player with a name and six starting Pokémon. name The player’s name. p1..p6 Six Pokémon to form the player’s initial team. @brief Records a victory against another trainer and collects their badge if they are a Gym Leader. enemy The trainer who was defeated.

docs.title: nickname
docs.text:
const std::string& nickname

@brief Gets the Pokémon’s name.

Return Const reference to the name string. @brief Gets the Pokémon’s primary type. Return The primary elemental type. @brief Gets the Pokémon’s secondary type. Return The secondary elemental type. @brief Gets the current hit points of the Pokémon. Return The current HP value. @brief Gets the Pokémon’s attack move name. Return Const reference to the attack name. @brief Gets the base damage value of the Pokémon’s attack. Return The attack damage integer. @brief Checks if the Pokémon is still alive. Return True if hp > 0, false otherwise. @brief Sets the Pokémon’s current hit points. hp New HP value to assign. @brief Interacts with the Pokémon, printing a catchphrase or petting message.

Special-cases Pikachu to say “Pika, pika !” and comments if HP is low.

docs.title: pokemon
docs.text:
Interactables::Pokemon& pokemon

@brief Gets the attack damage of the Pokémon at the given index, or 0 if the Pokémon is fainted.

index Zero-based index of the Pokémon whose attack damage to query. Return The attack damage value, or 0 if the Pokémon’s HP is -1 (indicating it cannot fight).

docs.title: pokemon
docs.text:
Interactables::Pokemon& pokemon

@brief Records a defeat against another trainer.

enemy The trainer who defeated this player. @brief Gets the list of badges the player has earned. Return Const reference to the vector of badge names. @brief Gets the number of fights the player has won. Return Number of victories. @brief Gets the number of fights the player has lost. Return Number of defeats. @brief Constructs a Master Trainer with a name and six powerful Pokémon. name The master trainer’s name. p1..p6 Six Pokémon to form the master’s team. @brief Calculates a boosted attack damage (125% of base) for master trainers. index Zero-based index of the Pokémon whose attack to boost. Return The boosted attack damage, or 0 if Pokémon is fainted.

docs.title: typeAttcker1
docs.text:
Pokemons::Type typeAttcker1

@brief Default constructs a Pokémon with placeholder values.

Initializes name to "Undefined", types to Normal, HP to -1 (undefined), attack to "Undefined", and damage to 0. @brief Constructs a Pokémon with the specified attributes.

name 
   The Pokémon’s name.
type1 
  Primary elemental type.
type2 
  Secondary elemental type.
hp 
     Initial and maximum hit points.
attack 
 Name of the attack move.
attackDamages Base damage value of the attack move. @brief Applies damage to this Pokémon based on type effectiveness. baseDamage The base damage of the incoming attack. attacker

The attacking Pokémon to determine type multipliers.

Prints a message indicating effectiveness, adjusts this.hp accordingly.

docs.title: operator<<
docs.text:
std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Pokemon& obj)

@brief Restores the Pokémon’s HP to its maximum value. @brief Serializes the Pokémon’s details to an output stream.

os The output stream to write to. obj The Pokémon instance to serialize. Return Reference to the output stream for chaining.

std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const AEntrainer& obj)

@brief Swaps two Pokémon in the team by their indices.

ia Index of the first Pokémon. ib Index of the second Pokémon. Return True if the swap succeeded (indices in range or ia == ib), false otherwise. @brief Default interaction behavior for an abstract trainer. Derived classes may override to provide custom dialogue or actions. @brief Serializes the trainer’s details and team to an output stream. os Output stream to write to. obj The trainer object to serialize. Return Reference to the output stream.

docs.title: AEntrainer
docs.text:
class AEntrainer: public IEntrainer

Maximum Team Size Current number of Pokemon in team @brief Adds a Pokemon to the trainer's team

pokemon The pokemon to add Return True if the Pokemon was successfully added (team not full) false otherwise @brief Removes a Pokemon from the trainer's team by it's index index Zero-based position of the Pokemon to remove Return True if removal succeeded, false otherwise @brief Retrieves a Pokemon from the team by its index index Zero-based position of the Pokemon to return Return A copy of the Pokemon at the given index @brief Gets the attack damage of a team mem

`` :

Return : @brief Abstract base trainer providing common implementation of IEntrainer.

Manages a team of up to six Pokémon, offers default add/remove, battle logic, and stream output. Concrete trainers should override interaction behavior and may specialize attack damage.

docs.title: Operators
docs.text: Name Description operator<< @brief Serializes trainer and team to an output stream.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Name Description AEntrainer @brief Default constructor initializes an empty trainer. addPokemon @brief Adds a Pokémon to the team if not full. rmPokemon @brief Removes a Pokémon from the team by index. getPokemon @brief Retrieves a Pokémon by index. getAllPokemons @brief Returns all Pokémon in the trainer’s team. fightAgainst @brief Executes a full battle sequence against another trainer. getPokemonAttackDamage @brief Gets the attack damage of a Pokémon, or 0 if fainted. switchPokemons @brief Swaps two Pokémon in the team. interact @brief Default interaction stub; override for custom behavior.

docs.title: operator<<
docs.text:
std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&, const AEntrainer&)

@brief Serializes trainer and team to an output stream.

os Output stream. obj Trainer instance to serialize. Return Reference to the output stream.

docs.text:
std::string lowerType

@brief Copy constructor for the Type wrapper.

other The Type instance to copy from. @brief Constructs a Type from a raw enum value. other The raw _Type enum value. @brief Constructs a Type by parsing its name. __type Case-insensitive string representing the type name, e.g. "Feu", "eau", "PLANTE".

Note

If the provided string does not match any known type, _type will remain uninitialized (consider handling unknown cases).

docs.title: operator<<
docs.text:
std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Type& obj)

@brief Retrieves the underlying raw enum value.

Return The raw Type::_Type held by this wrapper. @brief Inserts the Type’s name into an output stream. os The stream to write to. obj The Type instance whose name will be printed. Return Reference to the output stream.

docs.title: Type
docs.text:
class Type

@brief Enumeration of all possible Pokémon elemental types.

Eau Water type. Feu Fire type. Plante Grass type. Electrik Electric type. Glace Ice type. Combat Fighting type. Poison Poison type. Sol Ground type. Vol Flying type. Psy Psychic type. Insecte Bug type. Roche Rock type. Spectre Ghost type. Dragon Dragon type. Tenebre Dark type. Acier Steel type. Fee Fairy type. Normal Normal type. Undefined ¯_(ツ)_/¯
